rulings.Case Title: MARILOU D SAMSON VS. ONE WEST BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Of $18,424.00
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT ONE WEST BANK, N.A., ERRONEOUSLY SUED Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Of $18,424.00: Defendant's Motion for Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff filed and maintained an action not "warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification or reversal of existing law of the establishment of new law." Plaintiff's action was barred by res judicata ("[Th]he prior judgment is res judicata on matters which were raised or could have been raised, on matters litigate or litigable." Busick v. Workmen's Comp. Appeals Bd. (1972) 7 Cal.3d 967, 975. Plaintiff was self represented at the time the complaint was filed, at the time this motion was served and at the time the 21 days expired. Thus, Plaintiff does not fall with the CCP 127.7(d)(1) exception. Sanctions granted in the amount of $2,680.5 (Motion for Sanctions only). (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALLYSYN OVERTON VS. PATRICK LAKE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 2: PLAINTIFF ALLYSYN OVERTON'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED. Demurer to the first cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to attach 2011 contract as requested in the late-filed opposition. Demurer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant's late-filed opposition does not address arguments raised in the demurrer as to this cause of action. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDAN QUINLAN VS. JOHN PAXTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File A X-Complt
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, line 13. DEFENDANT JOHN PAXTON's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT DENIED. The delay in filing the cross-complaint is prejudicial. No evidence or legal authority is provided to support Defendant's contention that the cross-complaint is compulsory or that Plaintiff intends on dismissing the complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SIMPLEX INVESTMENT FUND I LLC VS. RONNIE MOREIRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT RONNIE MOREIRA'S Motion To Quash Summons: DENIED. Defendant moves to quash service of summons pursuant to CCP 414.10 and CCP 418.10. Neither statute requires that service be posted on the door. Defendant does not cite to CCP 415.45 in the moving papers and does not provide any evidence that CCP 415.45 and the Court's order of July 28, 2014 has not been complied with. Contrary to assertions in the Points and Authorities (page 3, lines 1-2) no declaration has been filed with the Court. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: W.J. BRITTON & CO., INC. VS. ADAN MEZA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Special Interoggs; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 3, Line 8: PLAINTIFF W.J. BRITTON & CO.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interogatories: GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide verified complete responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One; Form Interrogatories General, Set one; and Form Interrogatories-UD, Set One no later than September 5, 2014 by 4:00 p.m. by personal delivery. Sanctions granted against defendants and their counsel in the amount of $2,055. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: W.J. BRITTON & CO., INC. VS. ADAN MEZA ET AL

rulings.Case Title: TIPTON MANAGEMENT COMPANY VS. MANUEL FORD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT MANUEL FORD's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. RJN is GRANTED. Demurrer is solely based on an argument that DRS Management, not Tipton Management, is the property manager with capacity to sue. Complaint (and judicially noticeable documents) sufficiently and unambiguously alleges that Tipton Management in the property management, agent for the landlord and DRS Management is the landlord. Defendant improperly raises new argument in reply regarding Tipton Management's lack of capacity to sue as an agent for the landlord, DRS Management. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE BONIFACIO VS. JAMES E. CHRIST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, line 12. DEFENDANTs JAMES CHRIST and RUSSELL DEASON's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS' DENIED. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Extraneous issues introduced via defendant's declaration cannot be considered to determine sufficiency of the allegations and propriety of the five-day summons. = (501/REQ)
